title: Launching a Cloud Audit Log Platform for Healthcare Compliance

blocks.text: Led the 0→1 launch of a cloud audit logging platform — a system of record for regulated user, medication, and system activity across enterprise healthcare environments. Improved audit readiness, strengthened cloud adoption trust, and reduced modeled regulatory exposure by ~$2M.
Problem
Audit data was fragmented across products and systems.
Compliance teams relied on manual investigation to prepare for audits.
Enterprise customers needed stronger traceability before expanding cloud adoption.
Internal teams lacked shared definitions for what to log, retain, and expose.
My Role
Owned product strategy, MVP scope, and launch execution as Product Manager.
Partnered with Engineering, Security, Compliance, Legal, and Customer Success.
Aligned enterprise stakeholders on audit requirements and access patterns.
Product Approach
Scoped MVP to the highest-risk regulated workflows first.
Defined HIPAA-aligned event requirements with Security and Compliance.
Designed for self-serve audit review under real time pressure.
Balanced compliance rigor with engineering feasibility for a scalable launch.
Solution

items.title: Standardized event schema
items.description: Shared structure across services with room for context-specific metadata.

items.title: Secure submission APIs
items.description: Reliable ingestion for audit events from product services.

items.title: Audit search & filtering
items.description: Self-serve workflows for compliance and customer evidence retrieval.

items.title: Role-based access controls
items.description: Tight permissioning over sensitive audit data.

items.title: System of record
items.description: Durable architecture for regulated user and system activity.

blocks.text: Key Product Decisions

blocks.tradeoff: Highest-risk events first instead of full long-tail coverage.
blocks.outcome: Meaningful compliance value at launch without blocking on completeness.

blocks.tradeoff: Shared schema with service-specific metadata fields.
blocks.outcome: Consistent audit surface that still fit each service's domain.

blocks.tradeoff: Searchable workflows behind strict role-based access.
blocks.outcome: Compliance teams retrieved evidence directly without expanding risk.

blocks.tradeoff: Designed review around real investigation flows, not raw log dumps.
blocks.outcome: Adopted by teams under time pressure during audits.

blocks.text: Results & Impact
100% traceability for defined MVP security and compliance events.
~$2M modeled regulatory exposure reduced.
Audit evidence retrieval reduced from weeks to minutes.
Deployed across 3 major enterprise health systems.
Strengthened enterprise readiness for cloud expansion.
Created a scalable audit foundation for future platform growth.
Metrics Tracked
Audit event coverage for defined MVP workflows
Time to retrieve audit evidence
Services submitting audit events
Enterprise deployments
Compliance and security review readiness
Key Learnings
Strong platform products are often invisible — value comes from reducing risk and earning trust.
In regulated systems, the hard problem is aligning stakeholders on what 'complete' means.
Narrow MVP scope plus a scalable schema beats trying to log everything on day one.
